title: lac-claude — Claude Bridge
problem: Filling in feature fields (analysis, decisions, implementation notes) is high-value but time-consuming work. Without a programmatic Claude integration, every AI-assisted fill requires manually copying context into a chat window and pasting results back — defeating the purpose of structured provenance.
analysis: A private bridge library that connects feature.json files to the Anthropic API. Given a feature folder, it reads surrounding source files (up to ~200KB), builds a structured markdown context, and asks Claude to fill missing fields or generate code artifacts. Exposes two functions: fillFeature() for field completion and genFromFeature() for code generation. Used internally by both the CLI (lac fill) and the MCP server (fill_feature tool).
implementation: src/index.ts exports fillFeature() and genFromFeature(). context-builder.ts reads TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, and other source files adjacent to the feature.json to build the context payload. prompts.ts defines per-field system and user prompts for each fillable field (analysis, decisions, implementation, knownLimitations, tags, successCriteria, domain). The Anthropic SDK client in client.ts defaults to claude-sonnet-4-6. Responses for structured fields (JSON arrays/objects) are parsed after stripping markdown code fences. Interactive mode uses readline for field-by-field approval. Dry-run mode previews changes without writing.

decisions.decision: Per-field prompts rather than one prompt for all missing fields
decisions.rationale: Each field has a distinct shape and purpose. Separate prompts let Claude focus on one task at a time, producing higher-quality results than a single omnibus prompt.
alternativesConsidered: One prompt, all fields — faster but lower quality, harder to parse structured fields from a single response

decisions.decision: Read surrounding source files for context, not just feature.json
decisions.rationale: The best analysis and implementation notes come from reading the actual code, not just the feature metadata. Claude can infer decisions and limitations from the source.
alternativesConsidered: feature.json only — Claude would hallucinate plausible-sounding but inaccurate details

decisions.rationale: Best balance of capability and cost for the fill task. The context window is large enough to hold a typical feature folder's source files.
alternativesConsidered: Opus — more capable but 5x more expensive for a task where Sonnet is sufficient
Haiku — too fast, lower quality for nuanced analysis fields

knownLimitations: No caching — every fill call re-reads all source files and makes a fresh API call
Context truncation at ~200KB means very large feature folders may lose relevant source files
Prompt strings are hardcoded — no per-workspace customization of what Claude is asked to generate
readline interactive mode doesn't support terminal resize or advanced prompts
successCriteria: Running lac fill on a feature with missing fields produces accurate, non-hallucinated values for analysis, implementation, and knownLimitations that reflect the actual source code in the feature folder.

lineage.spawnReason: AI-assisted field filling is a core value proposition of the LAC workflow — this package is what makes it possible programmatically.
